source: PhysicallyBased API v2 (https://api.physicallybased.info/) and standard PBR references
notes: Albedo values are linear sRGB (0.0-1.0). Roughness uses the Disney/Unity convention (0=smooth mirror, 1=fully rough). Metalness is binary in physically-based rendering: 0 for non-metals, 1 for metals.

wood_oak.unityNotes: For a 1m^3 wooden cube prop, mass = 600 kg. Often too heavy for character interaction; consider scaling down or using a kinematic Rigidbody.

wood_pine.description: Polished pine wood surface (lighter than oak)

steel_polished.unityNotes: High density means even small steel objects are heavy. A 0.3m steel cube is ~212 kg.

iron_rusted.description: Rusted iron surface (partial metalness due to rust oxide layer)

gold_polished.description: Polished gold surface — highest metalness reflectance

rubber_black.unityNotes: Use for tires, soft body simulations. High friction PhysicMaterial recommended.

glass_clear.description: Clear glass — note low albedo since most light is transmitted, not reflected
glass_clear.unityNotes: Use a transparent shader. Standard PBR opaque shader will look wrong for glass.
